topic: ACT (Agentic Critical Training) integration into Squall

elevated.summary: Phase 1 git-diff correlation is architecturally invalid — no structured findings, semantic void
elevated.reasoning: ReviewModelResult stores unstructured text, no line metadata. Git-diff correlation requires mapping free-text to line changes — impossible without LLM-in-the-loop.

elevated.summary: DuckDB schema lacks finding-level granularity — tracks invocation telemetry, not semantic findings
elevated.reasoning: model_events has no finding_id, review_id, line ranges, or outcome labels. Massive schema overhaul needed.

elevated.summary: GRPO is wrong algorithm — need DPO for offline preference pairs
elevated.reasoning: Gemini: GRPO is online RL requiring policy to generate new responses. Historical pairs are offline preference data = DPO. Codex independently confirmed.

elevated.summary: Reward signal misspecified — acted_on conflates correctness with urgency/developer preference
elevated.reasoning: True but expensive findings get 'ignored'; trivial lint gets 'acted_on'. Training optimizes for compliance, not quality.

elevated.summary: Data volume insufficient by 10-100x for any ML training
elevated.reasoning: ~314 events, ~50-100 reviews. Need 5-10k pairs minimum for offline ranker, 20-50k for DPO.

dismissed.reasoning: All models acknowledged the conceptual mapping (evidence counting = discrete reward signal) is directionally correct. The idea isn't wrong, just the Layer 1 implementation.

user_correction: User pointed out the training data is at Layer 2 (conversation trajectory), not Layer 1 (DuckDB telemetry). The normal workflow (squall review -> 'you agree?' -> claude filters -> 'fix via TDD' -> commit) IS the trajectory. Models evaluated the wrong layer.
meta_insight: 5 models unanimously proved Layer 1 is flawed. User saw one meta-layer higher — the conversation itself is the training data. This is the most valuable training example: models were technically correct but strategically wrong because they couldn't see the layer they were embedded in.
